entities.name: European Council
entities.description: Strategic body composed of heads of state or government. Defines the general political direction and long-term priorities of the EU.

entities.name: European Commission
entities.description: Politically independent executive arm. Holds the monopoly on legislative initiative, executes the budget, and acts as guardian of the treaties.

entities.name: European Parliament
entities.description: Directly elected democratic body. Acts as a co-legislator, sharing the power to adopt and amend legislative proposals and the EU budget.

entities.name: Council of the European Union
entities.description: Intergovernmental body composed of national ministers. Shares legislative and budgetary authority with the Parliament.

entities.name: Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU)
entities.description: Supreme judicial authority. Ensures that EU legislation is interpreted and applied uniformly across all national legal systems.

entities.name: European Central Bank (ECB)
entities.description: Central monetary authority for the Eurozone. Manages the euro, frames economic policy, and ensures price stability.

entities.name: European Court of Auditors
entities.description: Independent assessment body that audits the revenue, expenditure, and financial management of the EU budget.

entities.name: Conciliation Committee
entities.description: Ad-hoc body comprising equal representatives from the Council and Parliament, tasked with drafting a joint text when co-legislators disagree.

entities.name: Decentralized EU Agencies
entities.description: Network of over 30 agencies providing specialized regulatory, technical, and scientific services to institutions and member states.

entities.name: Legislative Initiation and Executive Management
entities.description: Capability to translate strategic goals into actionable policy, propose laws, and ensure uniform implementation.

entities.name: Democratic Co-Legislation
entities.description: Capability to represent both the direct will of citizens and sovereign interests of member states in the creation of binding law.

entities.name: Judicial Arbitration and Enforcement
entities.description: Capability to ensure that EU law is interpreted and applied uniformly across all 27 distinct national legal systems.

entities.name: Monetary Stability Management
entities.description: Capability to manage monetary policy, maintain the stability of the Euro, and oversee banking supervision.

entities.name: Ordinary Legislative Procedure (OLP)
entities.description: Default process for adopting EU legislation, involving multiple readings and iterative negotiations between co-legislators.

relationships.name: Issues Strategic Mandates To
relationships.description: The European Council issues strategic guidelines and political direction to the European Commission.

relationships.name: Initiates Activity
relationships.description: The European Commission triggers the Ordinary Legislative Procedure by drafting a legislative proposal.

relationships.name: Submits Proposal To
relationships.description: The European Commission formally transmits legislative proposals to the European Parliament.

relationships.name: Submits Proposal To
relationships.description: The European Commission formally transmits legislative proposals to the Council of the European Union.

relationships.name: Exercises Political Control Over
relationships.description: The European Parliament holds the Commission accountable and possesses the power to pass a motion of censure.

relationships.name: Co-Legislates With
relationships.description: The European Parliament transmits its first/second reading positions and amendments to the Council of the EU.

relationships.name: Co-Legislates With
relationships.description: The Council of the EU transmits its first/second reading positions and counter-proposals to the European Parliament.

relationships.name: Mediates
relationships.description: The European Commission acts as a mediator within the Conciliation Committee to reconcile diverging positions.

relationships.name: Provides Audit Reports To
relationships.description: The Court of Auditors provides financial assessments to the Parliament for the annual budget discharge procedure.

relationships.name: Enforces Constraints On
relationships.description: The CJEU acts as the ultimate arbiter, adjudicating legal disputes involving the Commission, Parliament, and Member States.

relationships.name: Provides Specialized Services To
relationships.description: EU Agencies provide regulatory and scientific services to support the European Commission's executive functions.

relationships.name: Exhibits Capability
relationships.description: The European Commission exhibits the capability of Legislative Initiation and Executive Management.

relationships.name: Exhibits Capability
relationships.description: The European Parliament exhibits the capability of Democratic Co-Legislation.

relationships.name: Exhibits Capability
relationships.description: The Council of the European Union exhibits the capability of Democratic Co-Legislation.

relationships.name: Exhibits Capability
relationships.description: The CJEU exhibits the capability of Judicial Arbitration and Enforcement.

relationships.name: Exhibits Capability
relationships.description: The European Central Bank exhibits the capability of Monetary Stability Management.
